name: SheetJS (Excel/Spreadsheet I/O for JavaScript)
pricing_notes: Community edition: Apache-2.0 (0.18.x). Pro edition: paid. SheetJS LLC. Note: npm package frozen at 0.18.5 — get newer via CDN or sheetjs.com

performance.notes: Pure JavaScript; large files (>10MB XLSX) can be slow; streaming API for large files

agent_readiness.notes: Read/write Excel, CSV, ODS, and 20+ formats. const XLSX = require('xlsx'). Read: const wb = XLSX.readFile('file.xlsx'). const ws = wb.Sheets[wb.SheetNames[0]]. const data = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(ws). Write: const wb = XLSX.utils.book_new(). const ws = XLSX.utils.json_to_sheet([{name:'Alice', age:30}]). XLSX.utils.book_append_sheet(wb, ws, 'Sheet1'). XLSX.writeFile(wb, 'out.xlsx'). sheet_to_csv, sheet_to_html, sheet_to_formulae. aoa_to_sheet (array of arrays). cell styles require Pro. Streaming: XLSX.stream.to_csv(ws). Browser: FileReader + XLSX.read(data, {type:'binary'}).

gotchas: npm package (xlsx) frozen at 0.18.5 — SheetJS moved distribution to their own CDN
Cell styling/formatting requires Pro edition — community only does data
Security: known prototype pollution vulnerabilities in older versions — keep updated
Type coercion for dates can be surprising — use cellDates: true option
Large files need streaming API — default reads into memory
best_when: Reading and writing Excel files in Node.js or browser
Converting between Excel, CSV, JSON, and 20+ formats
Parsing user-uploaded Excel files in web apps
Generating Excel reports from JSON data
avoid_when: Cell styling/formatting (Community) — use ExcelJS for styling
Very large files without streaming — memory issues
When exceljs or xlsx-populate provides needed features without licensing questions
summary: SheetJS (xlsx npm) reads and writes Excel and 20+ formats. XLSX.readFile() / XLSX.writeFile(). sheet_to_json(), json_to_sheet(). Community: Apache-2.0 (frozen at 0.18.5 on npm). Pro for cell styling. Type-annotated. Best for Excel I/O in Node.js/browser. npm package is outdated — get from sheetjs.com. Security vulnerabilities in old versions. No cell styling in community. Use ExcelJS when styling is needed.
